_comment: Layer 3 subagent FAN-OUT verify (TIGHTENED for L4). orchestration='subagent-fanout' makes ONE lead agent delegate each sub-question to an ISOLATED sub-agent (sessions_spawn -> sessions_yield) so raw web pages stay in the child context and only a distilled summary returns to the lead — the STRUCTURAL bounded-context fix (vs Layer-1 pruning which trims in place). VERIFIED 2026-06-22 on L4/DiffusionGemma: lead spawned 2 isolated children, each ran real Brave web_search/web_fetch, raw pages stayed in children, lead synthesized a clean cited table (Python 3.14.6 / Node v24.15.0) in ~47s with compactionCount 0. timeout_seconds is intentionally SHORT (420s): the answer lands in <1 min, but `openclaw agent --local --json` then HANGS until killed (it doesn't self-exit while subagent children are reaped), so the remote recovers the synthesis from the server-side trajectory (_lead_synthesis_from_trajectory) instead of waiting for the CLI. Per-worker <=2 web_search/web_fetch cap lives in remote _fanout_lead_message. Launcher poll budget = step_timeout_seconds*steps+600 = 2000s > lead kill (timeout_seconds 420 + 120). Run: --gpu L4 --config configs/diffusiongemma_research.json.
steps: the current latest stable version of Python (from python.org)
the current latest LTS version of Node.js (from nodejs.org)
